key_points: Dorms available but early arrival policies are restrictive
International students face logistical challenges with move-in timing
Limited data from students on dorm quality or conditions
key_quotes: You're likely gonna have to find somewhere to stay until move-in opens
Do colleges usually allow students to arrive earlier by 4-5 days than the arrival date?

key_points: Campus life described as more lively than comparable LACs like Denison
International students worry about fitting into the social scene
Campus is open to the public, suggesting a welcoming, accessible environment
key_quotes: Campus life at Furman is a bit more lively than that of Denison
I'm kinda worried since I'm an international student and thus it would be harder for me to fit in
They are so gracious to open their campus to the public to enjoy

key_points: No specific student feedback on dining halls from any source
Cannot assess quality, hours, or variety from available data
Absence of complaints may indicate acceptable baseline experience
key_quotes: No dining-specific quotes available in the data

key_points: No student comments specifically address mental health resources
Academically rigorous environment may create pressure for pre-med students
Insufficient data to assess counseling or wellness support quality
key_quotes: No mental health-specific quotes available in the data

key_points: Total cost of attendance runs around $80k per year — very expensive
Merit scholarships like James B. Duke can significantly offset costs
International students report mixed aid generosity; appeals are possible
key_quotes: VERY expensive, and for the price it doesn't seem worth it
As it is shown on the website, tuition and fees come at around $80k
Is Furman University affordable? My budget is about $20k... Is it worth considering and maybe appealing more money?

key_points: Former admissions officer describes professors as approachable and supportive
Strong merit scholarship programs suggest academically competitive student body
3+2 engineering dual degree with Georgia Tech is an option but transfer rate unclear
key_quotes: I graduated from, and was an AO at, Furman. Shoot me a message and we can chat!
Do they have good STEM programs? Are the professors supportive and approachable?

key_points: Admissions office appears accessible — former AO actively helps students on Reddit
Waitlist process lacks clear instructions, causing confusion for applicants
Financial aid documents submitted late can delay decisions without clear communication
key_quotes: For Furman, they didn't have any further instructions
I submitted my financial documents a little later than the deadline, so I'm wondering if that might be causing a delay
I got my decision last week Friday! Waitlisted

key_points: Campus is widely praised as beautiful — a consistent standout feature
Located in Greenville, SC — described as a really nice city
Southern location may be a cultural adjustment for out-of-state or international students
key_quotes: Oh Furman is such a beautiful campus. So glad y'all got to tour it
I love Greenville though, it's a really nice city!
We're from Illinois, so we're going to have to be pretty selective about southern schools we actually visit

key_points: OPT/STEM designation is a concern for international students in finance or econ
No strong alumni network data or job placement statistics mentioned
3+2 GT partnership could boost engineering outcomes but acceptance rate is unknown
key_quotes: I care a lot about whether the programs are STEM-designated for OPT purposes as an international student
Can anyone who has gone through this type of transfer share the success rate, specifically the acceptance rate to Georgia Tech?

key_points: High sticker price of ~$80k raises serious value questions for many students
Strong merit aid can make it competitive but requires significant scholarship to justify cost
Multiple international students question whether Furman is worth $5k+ more than cheaper LACs
